steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question tests Words in Context. The word “comparatively” signals that the blank must contrast the Wi-Fi alternative with radar and sonar, which the passage explicitly calls “too costly for consumer use.” The blank must mean something close to low in cost.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — infrequent: Describes how often something occurs, not its cost. The passage says the Wi-Fi method would give users a “cheap way” to detect people — frequency is irrelevant here. ✗
  • Choice B — inexpensive: Directly contrasts with “too costly” and matches the passage’s own phrase “a cheap way.” Comparatively inexpensive correctly completes the cost comparison. ✓
  • Choice C — unmistakable: Means impossible to confuse or misidentify. Nothing in the passage compares the two technologies on the basis of clarity or distinctiveness. ✗
  • Choice D — impractical: The opposite of what the passage supports — the Wi-Fi modification is presented as a feasible consumer solution, not an unrealistic one. ✗
B is correct.
